description: The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The following recommendations should be considered while configuring the IP addresses for primary and backup controllers: While configuring a primary-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the primary cluster. Similarly, while configuring a backup-controller ip <ipv4-address>, it is recommended to use the IP address of a standalone controller or an IP address of a controller in the backup cluster. Configuring both primary or backup controller IP addresses from the same cluster is not supported.

description: User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Client IP Tracker?

Feature Caveat: Client IP Tracker - User-Based Tunneling (UBT) and DHCPv4/v6 snooping are mutually exclusive features in AOS-CX. When these two features are configured simultaneously, binding entries for the end clients will not be populated in the binding database. IP source-lockdown feature permits traffic from the clients to pass through the switch only if the client's MAC and IP addresses are present in the binding database. IP source lockdown can be enabled at both port and VLAN levels. The port-level configuration will be overridden by the VLAN-level configuration only when the port is a member of a single VLAN. If the port is a member of multiple VLANs, then the VLAN-level configuration for IP source lockdown will not be applied. Client IP tracker can track the IP address until the IP source lockdown is applied. Once the lockdown is in effect, the IP address will only be tracked if there is a valid entry in the binding table. However, if an IP address is already being tracked, it will remain active through probes, as probes utilize ARP packets, which are Layer 2 (L2) packets rather than Layer 3 (L3) packets.

description: Symptom: Link drops might be observed when the DAC cables, S1J07A or S1J08A is used for the products, S0E91A and S0X44A. Scenario: This issue is observed after upgrading the switches from 10.13.1000 or a later 10.13.1xxx version using VSF ISSU. Workaround: The products, S0E91A and S0X44A must be rebooted after an upgrade. Upgrade either the entire stack or only the stand alone switches based on your deployment. For stack with a mix of S0E91A, S0X44A, and other 6300 switches, either reboot the entire stack or perform ISSU and then manually reboot only the S0E91A and S0X44A switches using the vsf member <1-10> reboot command.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Physical Port?

Feature Caveat: Physical Port - Symptom: Link drops might be observed when the DAC cables, S1J07A or S1J08A is used for the products, S0E91A and S0X44A. Scenario: This issue is observed after upgrading the switches from 10.13.1000 or a later 10.13.1xxx version using VSF ISSU. Workaround: The products, S0E91A and S0X44A must be rebooted after an upgrade. Upgrade either the entire stack or only the stand alone switches based on your deployment. For stack with a mix of S0E91A, S0X44A, and other 6300 switches, either reboot the entire stack or perform ISSU and then manually reboot only the S0E91A and S0X44A switches using the vsf member <1-10> reboot command.

description: The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what limitation is mentioned for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - The switch does not support double encapsulation. A packet can be encapsulated with either L2GRE (UBT) or VXLAN, but not both. The network administrator should decide the tunneling type to be used, and then plan the configuration accordingly.

description: In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what additional caveat is documented for User Based Tunnel?

Feature Caveat: User Based Tunnel - In the event of license issues when onboarding a DUT to primary or backup mobility conductor, the DUT will not try to bootstrap to another mobility conductor where a license is available. For example, if the primary mobility conductor does not have a license to onboard the DUT but the backup mobility conductor does have adequate licenses, if both mobility conductors are reachable then UBT will be down, and the DUT will not attempt to bootstrap to the backup controller. However, if the primary mobility conductor is not reachable, the DUT gets tunneled to the standby/backup mobility conductor. Once the primary mobility conductor is reachable by the DUT once again, the DUT will not automatically bootstrap back to the primary. Network administrators should manually disable and enable UBT on the DUT to re-establish the tunnel to the primary mobility conductor.

description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: VXLAN - VXLAN encapsulation does not copy the ECN bits from inner header to outer header. If you create a traffic stream between connected hosts and enable the ECN bit for the hosts and start the traffic, a traffic capture may show that ECN bits arent copied from the inner header to the outer header.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - Boundary values for match vni and set local preference in a route-map system cannot be set via the REST API and must be manually configured on the switch via the CLI.

description: NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for ACLs?

Feature Caveat: ACLs - NOTE In a VSF stack, the switch may fail to log events for the matching access-list entries. ACL functionality is not impacted; access-list entries are applied properly and only the logging is incorrectly generated.

description: VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Aruba CX Mobile App?
Feature Caveat: Aruba CX Mobile App - VSF stack formation is blocked when there are reserved autojoin interfaces (25, 26, 49, 50) in the stack topology.

description: In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

messages.content: For 6300_6300L_6400 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - In environments with VRRP or VSX peers, while performing mutual route leaking on the VRRP peers with BGP neighborship established in between and towards the upstream network, the switch will install both routes as ECMP instead of preferring the leaked route. Use route-maps to give lower/higher preference to the routes received from an iBGP peer. For example: Copy !

description: Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal:6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns. ISSU Considerations: On the 6300 switch series, follow these additional steps: 1. After the upgrade, execute redundancy switchover (the conductor goes for a reboot and standby takes over as the new conductor). 2. After the old conductor rejoins the stack as new standby, execute redundancy switchover again.

description: Symptom: Users attempting to access the captive portal experience inconsistent behaviors such as: long delays (2-3 minutes) before the portal page loads, failure to display the portal page entirely, and certificate errors showing the switch’s local certificate. Scenario: When the captive portal service reaches its maximum session limits, the switch may fail to create new sessions, preventing new clients from being redirected properly.

description: Symptom: The Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. Scenario: This issue occurs when the NMS queries the switch using SNMP and relies on either of the following OIDs to retrieve IPv4 address information: 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.20.1.2 (deprecated) 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.34.1.5 (current but not accessible in 10.13) In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility.
symptom: The Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13.

description: Symptom: Multiple DFPD crashes seen on the switch when handling HTTP user agent information where the client generates specific HTTP request patterns with range downloads. description: Symptom: UBT client is not able to connect to UAC, causing it to remain in the Status: In Progress state. show ubt users all does not show the UBT tunnel as activated. Scenario:: This occurs when no enable is configured in one zone in a multi-zone configuration in the UBT switch. Workaround: Configure no enable in both zones, then configure enable.
symptom: UBT client is not able to connect to UAC, causing it to remain in the Status: In Progress state. show ubt users all does not show the UBT tunnel as activated.

workaround: Configure no enable in both zones, then configure enable.

description: Symptom: Some MAC addresses may not appear in the output of show mac-address-table. Scenario: In certain VXLAN deployments, this issue can occur when multiple MAC address updates are processed in quick succession. Workaround: Run clear mac-address-table to refresh the MAC table and restore missing entries.
